_meta.description: Curated historical lunar eclipses for validating the pure-tidal Moon model. Mirrors the structure of ECLIPSE_PRESETS (solar) embedded in src/script.js. Initial seed of 14 NASA-Lunar-Canon-verified events in the 2020-2025 window — the same events the Verify Lunar Eclipse Finder console test cross-checks the predictive finder against. Expansion to earlier eras is planned via Phase L-3 (NASA Lunar Canon import for -2000 to +3000 range) and via the Discover Lunar Eclipses console helper button.
_meta.history: 1.1 (2026-06-21): trimmed unverified Early-Modern/Medieval/Classical/Ancient draft entries that had mis-curated JDs (e.g. JD 2086877.5 labeled '1001 Sep 5' actually decodes to 1001 Jul 24). Catalog now contains only NASA-published JDs that the model's predictive finder agrees with to <2 days.

field_definitions.jd: Julian Date (UT) of greatest eclipse, from NASA Lunar Canon (Espenak/Meeus 2009).
field_definitions.label: Display string: 'YYYY Mon DD Type'.
field_definitions.magnitude_umbral: Fraction of Moon diameter within umbra at greatest eclipse. >1 for Total, (0,1) for Partial, 0 for Penumbral-only.
field_definitions.visibility: Continents/regions where Moon was above horizon during eclipse (lunar eclipses are visible from entire night-side hemisphere — much broader than solar paths).

sources: NASA Five Millennium Canon of Lunar Eclipses (Espenak & Meeus 2009)
Said, S.S. & Stephenson, F.R. (1996). Solar and Lunar Eclipse Measurements by Medieval Muslim Astronomers, I: Background. J. Hist. Astronomy 27, 259-273.
Stephenson, F.R. (1997). Historical Eclipses and Earth's Rotation.
expansion_workflow: 1. Run console test 'Discover lunar eclipses in a year range' (default 1000-1010 CE) to list every model-predicted opposition.
2. Cross-reference candidate JDs against NASA Lunar Canon and Stephenson 1997 to confirm the event was historically observed/documented.
3. Add the verified entry to LUNAR_ECLIPSE_PRESETS in src/script.js and this JSON.
4. Run console test 'Validate LUNAR_ECLIPSE_PRESETS catalog entries' to confirm JD↔label↔model match.
